self.name: Disable Accounts for Detected Outlier Identities (0.9+)a

object.name: Disable Accounts for Detected Outlier Identities (0.9+)a
object.description: A nine-step workflow to trigger when a detected outlier score is at or above 0.9. When the score is this high, all accounts for the identity are disabled and an email notification is sent to their manager to review access and investigate the exceptions.

Compare Numbers.description: The workflow triggers at or above 0.9. This comparator checks the value to ensure it is at or above 0.9.

Get Access.description: This action returns all access associated with the outlier identity.

Get Accounts.description: This action is used to gather all the accounts associated with the identity. In this workflow, the accounts will be disabled until a review can be conducted.

Get Identity.description: This action returns attributes associated with the identity that triggered the outlier score.

Get Identity 1.description: This action returns the outlier identity's manager's name and attributes.

Manage Accounts.description: This action disables all account found in the prior action "Get Accounts".

attributes.body:
Dear ${manager},

The SailPoint AI Engine has detected a user that has outlier access resulting in excessive risk. This identity, ${user} , is your direct report and has an outlier score of ${score}.

Please note that all accounts for ${user} have been disabled until a full forensic audit can be completed. A complete listing of all access can be found below.


Thank you,
Your Security & Compliance Team.

Access:
${access}
attributes.subject: Outlier - Excessive Risk notification

Send Email.description: This action sends an email notification to the outlier identity's manager. The email informs the manager that the identity had an outlier score that was deemed "Excessive Risk" and action has been taken to disable all accounts until a full security review can be completed.

success.description: The outlier score was not at or above 0.9. No further action is required.
